revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:愛宕信仰]]
[[File:Torii Atago-shrine Kyoto.JPG|thumb|250px|{{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}}]] [[File:Atago_Gongen.png|thumb|{{ill|Atago Gongen|en|Atago Gongen|qid=Q1101132|lt=Atago Gongen|1=Atago Gongen}}. From the Buddhist Iconography (仏像図彙), 1783.]] '''Atago Faith''' (愛宕信仰, Atago Shinkō) is a Shinto belief in the fire prevention deity originating from {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}}, located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Atago|en|Mount Atago|qid=Q2975204|lt=Mount Atago|1=Mount Atago}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. Across Japan, approximately 1,000 shrines in 43 prefectures bear the name "Atago" as part of their title.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=The Origin of the Fire Prevention Deity "Atago" — Visiting Japan's Three Major Atago Shrines|url=https://www.fdma.go.jp/publication/ugoki/assets/2702_03.pdf|publisher=Fire and Disaster Management Agency|accessdate=2023-01-15|qq=}}</ref> These shrines are particularly common in the Tōhoku region. The enshrined deities are typically {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} or {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}.<ref name=":0" /> == History == Atago Shrine on Mount Atago has long served as a center for {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}},<ref name=":0" /> and during the medieval period, Atago Shrine was established there.<ref name=":0" /> Practitioners of Shugendō are believed to have spread Atago Faith nationwide.<ref name=":0" /> During the Sengoku period, the deity {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu#Relationship to Dōsojin|ja|地蔵菩薩#道祖神との関係|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu#Relationship to Dōsojin|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q604687}} was revered as the honjibutsu (Buddhist counterpart) of the Atago deity, leading to the belief in the deity as a god of military victory.<ref name="名前なし-20230316122012">{{Cite journal|author=Tomoko Harashima|date=2006-03|title=Regional Development of Atago Faith: Focusing on the Area Around Shiroishi City, Miyagi Prefecture|url=https://archives.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/repository/baker/rid_AS000200005277|journal=Research Bulletin of the Buddhist University Asia Religious and Cultural Information Research Institute|volume=2|pages=237-251|publisher=Buddhist University Asia Religious and Cultural Information Research Institute|qq=}}</ref> Warriors revered Atago Shrine for ensuring victory, and over time, the victory god was reinterpreted as a fire prevention deity. Warriors from various domains carried this belief back to their local regions during their sankin-kōtai (alternate attendance) journeys, leading to the establishment of Atago shrines across Japan.<ref name=":0" /> For instance, the shift from military god worship to fire prevention worship at Kyoto's Mount Atago is attributed to the Edo period.<ref name="名前なし-20230316122012" /> {{citation needed span|Not only {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu|1=Kṣitigarbha}}, but also {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} (also known as {{ill|Homusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|火産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502344|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) were enshrined together. Even today, the {{ill|Ennichi|en|Ennichi|qid=Q985655|lt=Ennichi|1=Ennichi}} (festivals) of Atago coincide with Jizō's on the 24th of each month. In the Edo period, "Atago-kō" associations were formed across Japan, and it was believed that visiting Atago Shrine on August 1st, called the "Thousand Day Pilgrimage," was equivalent to visiting the shrine for a thousand days.|date=2023-01|qq=}} One theory for {{ill|Naoe Kanetsugu|en|Naoe Kanetsugu|qid=Q1348611|lt=Naoe Kanetsugu|1=Naoe Kanetsugu}} wearing a front crest with the character for "Ai" (愛, love) on his helmet suggests it was linked to his devotion to Atago Faith.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20090509144803/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/culture/academic/090506/acd0905060832001-n3.htm Understanding Naoe Kanetsugu: Unveiling Mysteries of a Loyal and Intelligent General (3/4)] Sankei News, May 6, 2009.</ref> == Major Atago Shrines == ;Head Shrine *{{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}) - Former Provincial Shrine. ;Hokkaidō and Tōhoku Region *{{ill|Atago Shrine (Yuzawa)|ja|愛宕神社 (湯沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Yuzawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502345|1=Atago Shrine (Yuzawa)}} ({{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yuzawa, Akita|en|Yuzawa, Akita|qid=Q633272|lt=Yuzawa|1=Yuzawa, Akita}}) - Former Prefectural Shrine. *{{ill|Atago Shrine (Tendō)|ja|愛宕神社 (天童市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Tendō)|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tendō, Yamagata|en|Tendō, Yamagata|qid=Q385779|lt=Tendō|1=Tendō, Yamagata}}) - Former Prefectural Shrine. ;Kinki Region *{{ill|Atago Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|愛宕神社 (亀岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Kameoka)|qq=}} ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|en|Kameoka, Kyoto|qid=Q633996|lt=Kameoka|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}) - Original Atago Shrine. ;Kyushu and Okinawa Region *{{ill|Atago Shrine (Miyazaki)|ja|愛宕神社 (宮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Miyazaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502347|1=Atago Shrine (Miyazaki)}} ({{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Miyazaki (city)|en|Miyazaki (city)|qid=Q204308|lt=Miyazaki|1=Miyazaki (city)}}) - Former Prefectural Shrine. For other shrines named "Atago Shrine," see {{ill|Atago Shrine|en|Atago Shrine|qid=Q11493863|lt=Atago Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Atago Shrine}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == *{{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋葉権現}} *{{ill|Mount Atago|ja|愛宕山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Atago|1=Mount Atago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493827}} *{{ill|Atago Gongen|en|Atago Gongen|qid=Q1101132|lt=Atago Gongen|1=Atago Gongen}} *{{ill|Atago Matsuri|ja|愛宕祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Matsuri|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Buddhism-stub|qq=}} {{Atago Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|愛宕信仰|version=102996429|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:愛宕信仰]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11493823}} [[Category:愛宕信仰]] [[Category:Fire prevention rituals]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]]
